sanskrit_devanagari:
ये त्वेतदभ्यसूयन्तो नानुतिष्ठन्ति मे मतम् ।
सर्वज्ञानविमूढांस्तान्विद्धि नष्टानचेतसः ॥ ३२ ॥
sanskrit_iast:
ye tv etad abhyasūyanto
nānutiṣṭhanti me matam |
sarva-jñāna-vimūḍhāṁs tān
viddhi naṣṭān acetasaḥ || 32 ||

sanskrit_sources.agreement: exact (raw HTML scrape; akṣara sequence as published)
exact (text body identical with vedabase; punctuation rendering differs)
exact (text body identical; punctuation rendering differs)
exact (academic critical edition; cross-reference Bhg_03.032 = MBh_06,025.032). Body text identical; word-segmentation differs by edition.

translations.verbatim_quote: But those who, out of envy, disregard these teachings and do not follow them regularly are to be considered bereft of all knowledge, befooled, and ruined in their endeavors for perfection.
(See holy-bhagavad-gita.org for full Mukundananda rendering; agreement with Prabhupada noted in disagreements section where it diverges materially.)

commentaries.verbatim_excerpt: The flaw of not being Kṛṣṇa conscious is clearly stated herein. As there is punishment for disobedience to the order of the supreme executive head, so there is certainly punishment for disobedience to the order of the Supreme Personality of Godhead.

commentaries.translator: Swami Gambhirananda (Advaita Ashrama edition of Sankara-bhasya, as reproduced on bhagavad-gita.us)
commentaries.verbatim_excerpt: 3.32 Tu, but; ye, those who are the opposite of them (the former); who abhyasuyantah, decrying; etat, this instruction of Mine; na, do not; anutisthanti, follow; me, My; matam, teaching, they are deluded in various ways with respect to all knowledge.
commentaries.summary_paraphrase: Shankara: those who, abhyasūyantaḥ — finding fault with — me matam — my teaching — na anutiṣṭhanti — do not practice it — viddhi — know them — as sarva-jñāna-vimūḍhān (deluded as to all knowledge), naṣṭān (lost), acetasaḥ (devoid of discrimination). The verdict is severe and total: rejection of the teaching is not just rejection of one teaching among many but a state of comprehensive delusion.

commentaries.translator: Swami Adidevananda (Sri Ramakrishna Math edition of Ramanuja's Gita-bhasya, as reproduced on bhagavad-gita.us)
commentaries.verbatim_excerpt: 3.32 But those who do not follow this view of Mine, namely, that the self has Me for Its support, is subservient to Me and is actuated by Me only, i.e., those who do not perform all acts contemplating in this way as also those who have no faith in the meaning of the Sastras and ca
commentaries.summary_paraphrase: Ramanuja: those who find fault with this teaching and do not implement it become fully deluded with respect to scriptural knowledge generally; they are lost — outside the liberative chain — and mindless. The verse establishes the teaching's centrality to the path: rejection is not neutral but actively destructive of the discriminating function.

positions.rendering: but those who, out of envy, disregard these teachings and do not practice them regularly are to be considered bereft of all knowledge, befooled, and ruined in their endeavors for perfection
but those who find fault with My teachings, being bereft of knowledge and devoid of discrimination, they disregard these principles and bring about their own ruin

abhyasūyantaḥ — those who, finding fault with the teaching, do not implement it — are sarva-jñāna-vimūḍhān (deluded as to ALL knowledge, not just this one), naṣṭān (lost from the path entirely), acetasaḥ (devoid of discrimination)

the fault-finding non-implementation results in being wholly deluded with regard to scriptural knowledge; such persons are 'lost' — outside the chain of liberation — and 'mindless' — operating without the discriminating function

disagreements_among_translators.explanation: Both commentators emphasise that the verse's verdict is total — sarva-jñāna-vimūḍhān (deluded as to ALL knowledge, not just this teaching), naṣṭān (lost in the strong sense), acetasaḥ (mindless). Translators sometimes soften this to 'misguided' or 'befooled' but the Sanskrit is severe. The pair 3.31-3.32 is doctrinally sharp: faith-and-no-cavil → release; cavil-and-no-implementation → total loss.

literal_meaning: But those who, finding fault with this teaching of mine, do not practice it — know them as deluded with regard to all knowledge, lost, mindless.
traditional_meaning_consensus: The contrapositive of 3.31. Those who object to the teaching (abhyasūyantaḥ) and do not practice it (na anutiṣṭhanti me matam) are sarva-jñāna-vimūḍhān (deluded with respect to all knowledge), naṣṭān (lost), acetasaḥ (mindless/devoid of discrimination). The verse establishes that the teaching is not optional for the seeker; rejection of it is itself a form of self-loss.
